forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system over the Tasman Sea will maintain a ridge across South Australia today. Northerly winds freshen today ahead of a front that is forecast to move across the far west this afternoon, then continue across the state during the weekend. The low pressure system associated with this front is expected to linger over waters to the south of the state resulting in a trough moving over southern parts later Sunday and Monday. The low will then move into Bass Strait on Monday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers and possible thunderstorms developing west of about Ceduna. Maximum temperatures well above average. Northerly winds freshening with southwesterly change developing in the west during the afternoon and evening.

A band of patchy rain with possible gusty thunderstorms about the west will move eastwards to reach eastern border districts during the afternoon. Isolated showers persisting about western and southern coasts. Maximum temperatures well above average with fresh and gusty northerly winds ahead of a west to southwesterly change in the west moving east to reach eastern border districts in the afternoon and evening, bringing below average temperatures.

Showers about the agricultural areas with possible thunderstorms mainly about coasts and ranges. Patchy rain and thunderstorms clearing from the northeast early. Maximum temperatures generally below average, grading to near average about the eastern border. Fresh west to southwesterly winds extending throughout, becoming gusty at times near the coasts.

text.p: Showers about the agricultural areas, possibly extending into the south of the pastoral districts. Showers then mostly contracting to coastal parts during the evening. Maximum temperatures below average. West to southwesterly winds, gusty at times near southern coasts.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Monday: generally less than 2 mm across the north pastoral districts, increasing to 2 to 10 mm about the south of the pastoral districts and agricultural areas. Scattered areas of 5 to 15 mm across the West Coast district and southern agricultural areas with isolated higher falls of 15 to 30 mm possible over parts of the Mount Lofty Ranges, western and far southern coastal fringes.
